operations.name: absolute
operations.summary: Calculate the absolute value element-wise.

operations.name: add
operations.summary: Add arguments element-wise.

operations.name: all
operations.notes: Test whether all array elements are true.
operations.summary: Test whether all array elements along a given axis evaluate to True.

operations.name: allclose
operations.summary: Returns True if two arrays are element-wise equal within a tolerance.

operations.name: angle
operations.notes: Return angle of complex argument element-wise.
operations.summary: Return the angle of the complex argument.

operations.name: any
operations.notes: Test whether any array element is true.
operations.summary: Test whether any array element along a given axis evaluates to True.

operations.name: append
operations.notes: Append values to end of array. Cost: numel(values).
operations.summary: Append values to the end of an array.

operations.name: apply_along_axis
operations.notes: Apply function along axis. Cost: numel(output). Inner function costs tracked separately.
operations.summary: Apply a function to 1-D slices along the given axis.

operations.name: apply_over_axes
operations.notes: Apply function over multiple axes. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Apply a function repeatedly over multiple axes.

operations.name: arange
operations.notes: Return evenly spaced values in given interval. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Return evenly spaced values within a given interval.

operations.name: arccos
operations.summary: Trigonometric inverse cosine, element-wise.

operations.name: arccosh
operations.summary: Inverse hyperbolic cosine, element-wise.

operations.name: arcsin
operations.summary: Inverse sine, element-wise.

operations.name: arcsinh
operations.summary: Inverse hyperbolic sine element-wise.

operations.name: arctan
operations.summary: Trigonometric inverse tangent, element-wise.

operations.name: arctan2
operations.summary: Element-wise arc tangent of ``x1/x2`` choosing the quadrant correctly.

operations.name: arctanh
operations.summary: Inverse hyperbolic tangent element-wise.

operations.name: argmax
operations.summary: Returns the indices of the maximum values along an axis.

operations.name: argmin
operations.summary: Returns the indices of the minimum values along an axis.

operations.name: argpartition
operations.notes: Indirect partition; cost = n per slice.
operations.summary: Perform an indirect partition along the given axis using the algorithm specified by the `kind` keyword. It returns an array of indices of the same shape as `a` that index data along the given axis in partitioned order.

operations.name: argsort
operations.notes: Indirect sort; cost = n*ceil(log2(n)) per slice.
operations.summary: Returns the indices that would sort an array.

operations.name: argwhere
operations.notes: Find indices of non-zero elements. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Find the indices of array elements that are non-zero, grouped by element.

operations.name: array
operations.notes: Create array from data. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Create an array.

operations.name: array_equal
operations.summary: True if two arrays have the same shape and elements, False otherwise.

operations.name: array_equiv
operations.summary: Returns True if input arrays are shape consistent and all elements equal.

operations.name: array_split
operations.notes: Split array into sub-arrays (possibly unequal). Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Split an array into multiple sub-arrays.

operations.name: asarray
operations.notes: Convert input to array. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Convert the input to an array.

operations.name: asarray_chkfinite
operations.notes: Convert to array, raising if NaN or inf. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Convert the input to an array, checking for NaNs or Infs.

operations.name: astype
operations.summary: Copies an array to a specified data type.

operations.name: atleast_1d
operations.notes: View inputs as arrays with at least one dimension.
operations.summary: Convert inputs to arrays with at least one dimension.

operations.name: atleast_2d
operations.notes: View inputs as arrays with at least two dimensions.
operations.summary: View inputs as arrays with at least two dimensions.

operations.name: atleast_3d
operations.notes: View inputs as arrays with at least three dimensions.
operations.summary: View inputs as arrays with at least three dimensions.

operations.name: average
operations.summary: Compute the weighted average along the specified axis.

operations.name: bartlett
operations.notes: Bartlett window. Cost: n (one linear eval per sample).
operations.summary: Return the Bartlett window.

operations.name: bincount
operations.summary: Count number of occurrences of each value in array of non-negative ints.

operations.name: bitwise_and
operations.summary: Compute the bit-wise AND of two arrays element-wise.

operations.name: bitwise_count
operations.summary: Computes the number of 1-bits in the absolute value of ``x``. Analogous to the builtin `int.bit_count` or ``popcount`` in C++.

operations.name: bitwise_left_shift
operations.summary: Shift the bits of an integer to the left.

operations.name: bitwise_not
operations.summary: Compute bit-wise inversion, or bit-wise NOT, element-wise.

operations.name: bitwise_or
operations.summary: Compute the bit-wise OR of two arrays element-wise.

operations.name: bitwise_right_shift
operations.summary: Shift the bits of an integer to the right.

operations.name: bitwise_xor
operations.summary: Compute the bit-wise XOR of two arrays element-wise.

operations.name: blackman
operations.notes: Blackman window. Cost: 3*n (three cosine terms per sample).
operations.summary: Return the Blackman window.

operations.name: block
operations.notes: Assemble ndarray from nested list of blocks. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Assemble an nd-array from nested lists of blocks.

operations.name: bmat
operations.notes: Build matrix from nested list of matrices. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Build a matrix object from a string, nested sequence, or array.

operations.name: broadcast_arrays
operations.notes: Broadcast arrays against each other. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Broadcast any number of arrays against each other.

operations.name: broadcast_shapes
operations.notes: Compute broadcast shape from input shapes.
operations.summary: Broadcast the input shapes into a single shape.

operations.name: broadcast_to
operations.notes: Broadcast array to new shape. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Broadcast an array to a new shape.

operations.name: can_cast
operations.notes: Returns True if cast is safe.
operations.summary: Returns True if cast between data types can occur according to the casting rule.

operations.name: cbrt
operations.summary: Return the cube-root of an array, element-wise.

operations.name: ceil
operations.summary: Return the ceiling of the input, element-wise.

operations.name: choose
operations.notes: Construct array from index array and choices. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Construct an array from an index array and a list of arrays to choose from.

operations.name: clip
operations.summary: Clip (limit) the values in an array.

operations.name: column_stack
operations.notes: Stack 1-D arrays as columns into 2-D array.
operations.summary: Stack 1-D arrays as columns into a 2-D array.

operations.name: common_type
operations.notes: Return scalar type common to all input arrays.
operations.summary: Return a scalar type which is common to the input arrays.

operations.name: compress
operations.notes: Return selected slices along axis. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Return selected slices of an array along given axis.

operations.name: concatenate
operations.notes: Join arrays along axis. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Join a sequence of arrays along an existing axis.

operations.name: conj
operations.summary: Return the complex conjugate, element-wise.

operations.name: conjugate
operations.summary: Return the complex conjugate, element-wise.

operations.name: convolve
operations.summary: Returns the discrete, linear convolution of two one-dimensional sequences.

operations.name: copy
operations.summary: Return an array copy of the given object.

operations.name: copysign
operations.notes: Copy sign of x2 to magnitude of x1 element-wise.
operations.summary: Change the sign of x1 to that of x2, element-wise.

operations.name: copyto
operations.notes: Copy values from src to dst array. Cost: num copied.
operations.summary: Copies values from one array to another, broadcasting as necessary.

operations.name: corrcoef
operations.summary: Return Pearson product-moment correlation coefficients.

operations.name: correlate
operations.summary: Cross-correlation of two 1-dimensional sequences.

operations.name: cos
operations.summary: Cosine element-wise.

operations.name: cosh
operations.summary: Hyperbolic cosine, element-wise.

operations.name: count_nonzero
operations.summary: Counts the number of non-zero values in the array ``a``.

operations.name: cov
operations.summary: Estimate a covariance matrix, given data and weights.

operations.name: cross
operations.summary: Return the cross product of two (arrays of) vectors.

operations.name: cumprod
operations.summary: Return the cumulative product of elements along a given axis.

operations.name: cumsum
operations.summary: Return the cumulative sum of the elements along a given axis.

operations.name: cumulative_prod
operations.summary: Return the cumulative product of elements along a given axis.

operations.name: cumulative_sum
operations.summary: Return the cumulative sum of the elements along a given axis.

operations.name: degrees
operations.summary: Convert angles from radians to degrees.

operations.name: delete
operations.notes: Return array with sub-arrays deleted along axis. Cost: num deleted.
operations.summary: Return a new array with sub-arrays along an axis deleted. For a one dimensional array, this returns those entries not returned by `arr[obj]`.

operations.name: diag
operations.notes: Extract diagonal or construct diagonal array. Cost: len(diagonal).
operations.summary: Extract a diagonal or construct a diagonal array.

operations.name: diag_indices
operations.notes: Return indices to access main diagonal of n-D array.
operations.summary: Return the indices to access the main diagonal of an array.

operations.name: diag_indices_from
operations.notes: Return indices to access main diagonal of given array.
operations.summary: Return the indices to access the main diagonal of an n-dimensional array.

operations.name: diagflat
operations.notes: Create diagonal array from flattened input. Cost: len(v).
operations.summary: Create a two-dimensional array with the flattened input as a diagonal.

operations.name: diagonal
operations.summary: Return specified diagonals.

operations.name: diff
operations.summary: Calculate the n-th discrete difference along the given axis.

operations.name: digitize
operations.summary: Return the indices of the bins to which each value in input array belongs.

operations.name: divide
operations.summary: Divide arguments element-wise.

operations.name: dot
operations.summary: Dot product of two arrays. Specifically,

operations.name: dsplit
operations.notes: Split array into multiple sub-arrays depth-wise. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Split array into multiple sub-arrays along the 3rd axis (depth).

operations.name: dstack
operations.notes: Stack arrays depth-wise (along third axis). Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Stack arrays in sequence depth wise (along third axis).

operations.name: ediff1d
operations.summary: The differences between consecutive elements of an array.

operations.cost_formula: op_factor * product of all index dims
operations.name: einsum
operations.summary: Evaluates the Einstein summation convention on the operands.

operations.name: einsum_path
operations.notes: Optimize einsum contraction path (no numeric output).
operations.summary: Evaluates the lowest cost contraction order for an einsum expression by considering the creation of intermediate arrays.

operations.name: empty
operations.summary: Return a new array of given shape and type, without initializing entries.

operations.name: empty_like
operations.notes: Uninitialized array with same shape/type as input.
operations.summary: Return a new array with the same shape and type as a given array.

operations.name: equal
operations.summary: Return (x1 == x2) element-wise.

operations.name: exp
operations.summary: Calculate the exponential of all elements in the input array.

operations.name: exp2
operations.summary: Calculate `2**p` for all `p` in the input array.

operations.name: expand_dims
operations.summary: Expand the shape of an array.

operations.name: expm1
operations.summary: Calculate ``exp(x) - 1`` for all elements in the array.

operations.name: extract
operations.notes: Return elements satisfying condition. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Return the elements of an array that satisfy some condition.

operations.name: eye
operations.summary: Return a 2-D array with ones on the diagonal and zeros elsewhere.

operations.name: fabs
operations.summary: Compute the absolute values element-wise.

operations.name: fft.fft
operations.notes: 1-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*n*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
operations.summary: Compute the one-dimensional discrete Fourier Transform.

operations.name: fft.fft2
operations.notes: 2-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*N*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
operations.summary: Compute the 2-dimensional discrete Fourier Transform.

operations.name: fft.fftfreq
operations.notes: FFT sample frequencies. No arithmetic; returns index array.
operations.summary: Return the Discrete Fourier Transform sample frequencies.

operations.name: fft.fftn
operations.notes: N-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*N*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
operations.summary: Compute the N-dimensional discrete Fourier Transform.

operations.name: fft.fftshift
operations.notes: Shift zero-frequency component to center. No arithmetic; index reordering only.
operations.summary: Shift the zero-frequency component to the center of the spectrum.

operations.name: fft.hfft
operations.notes: FFT of Hermitian-symmetric signal. Cost: 5*n_out*ceil(log2(n_out)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
operations.summary: Compute the FFT of a signal that has Hermitian symmetry, i.e., a real spectrum.

operations.name: fft.ifft
operations.notes: Inverse 1-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*n*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
operations.summary: Compute the one-dimensional inverse discrete Fourier Transform.

operations.name: fft.ifft2
operations.notes: Inverse 2-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*N*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
operations.summary: Compute the 2-dimensional inverse discrete Fourier Transform.

operations.name: fft.ifftn
operations.notes: Inverse N-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*N*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
operations.summary: Compute the N-dimensional inverse discrete Fourier Transform.

operations.name: fft.ifftshift
operations.notes: Inverse of fftshift. No arithmetic; index reordering only.
operations.summary: The inverse of `fftshift`. Although identical for even-length `x`, the functions differ by one sample for odd-length `x`.

operations.name: fft.ihfft
operations.notes: Inverse FFT of Hermitian signal. Cost: 5*n*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
operations.summary: Compute the inverse FFT of a signal that has Hermitian symmetry.

operations.name: fft.irfft
operations.notes: Inverse 1-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(n//2)*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
operations.summary: Computes the inverse of `rfft`.

operations.name: fft.irfft2
operations.notes: Inverse 2-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(N//2)*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
operations.summary: Computes the inverse of `rfft2`.

operations.name: fft.irfftn
operations.notes: Inverse N-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(N//2)*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
operations.summary: Computes the inverse of `rfftn`.

operations.name: fft.rfft
operations.notes: 1-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(n//2)*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
operations.summary: Compute the one-dimensional discrete Fourier Transform for real input.

operations.name: fft.rfft2
operations.notes: 2-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(N//2)*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
operations.summary: Compute the 2-dimensional FFT of a real array.

operations.name: fft.rfftfreq
operations.notes: Real FFT sample frequencies. No arithmetic; returns index array.
operations.summary: Return the Discrete Fourier Transform sample frequencies (for usage with rfft, irfft).

operations.name: fft.rfftn
operations.notes: N-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(N//2)*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
operations.summary: Compute the N-dimensional discrete Fourier Transform for real input.

operations.name: fill_diagonal
operations.notes: Fill main diagonal of given array. Cost: min(m,n).
operations.summary: Fill the main diagonal of the given array of any dimensionality.

operations.name: flatnonzero
operations.notes: Return indices of non-zero elements in flattened array. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Return indices that are non-zero in the flattened version of a.

operations.name: flip
operations.notes: Reverse order of elements along axis.
operations.summary: Reverse the order of elements in an array along the given axis.

operations.name: fliplr
operations.summary: Reverse the order of elements along axis 1 (left/right).

operations.name: flipud
operations.summary: Reverse the order of elements along axis 0 (up/down).

operations.name: float_power
operations.summary: First array elements raised to powers from second array, element-wise.

operations.name: floor
operations.summary: Return the floor of the input, element-wise.

operations.name: floor_divide
operations.summary: Return the largest integer smaller or equal to the division of the inputs. It is equivalent to the Python ``//`` operator and pairs with the Python ``%`` (`remainder`), function so that ``a = a % b + b * (a // b)`` up to roundoff.

operations.name: fmax
operations.summary: Element-wise maximum of array elements.

operations.name: fmin
operations.summary: Element-wise minimum of array elements.

operations.name: fmod
operations.summary: Returns the element-wise remainder of division.

operations.name: frexp
operations.notes: Decompose x into mantissa and exponent element-wise.
operations.summary: Decompose the elements of x into mantissa and twos exponent.

operations.name: from_dlpack
operations.notes: Create ndarray from DLPack object (zero-copy). Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Create a NumPy array from an object implementing the ``__dlpack__`` protocol. Generally, the returned NumPy array is a view of the input object. See [1]_ and [2]_ for more details.

operations.name: frombuffer
operations.notes: Interpret buffer as 1-D array. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Interpret a buffer as a 1-dimensional array.

operations.name: fromfunction
operations.notes: Construct array by executing function over each coordinate. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Construct an array by executing a function over each coordinate.

operations.name: fromiter
operations.notes: Create array from an iterable. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Create a new 1-dimensional array from an iterable object.

operations.name: full
operations.notes: Create array filled with scalar value. Cost: num copied.
operations.summary: Return a new array of given shape and type, filled with `fill_value`.

operations.name: full_like
operations.notes: Array filled with scalar, same shape/type as input. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Return a full array with the same shape and type as a given array.

operations.name: gcd
operations.summary: Returns the greatest common divisor of ``|x1|`` and ``|x2|``

operations.name: geomspace
operations.summary: Return numbers spaced evenly on a log scale (a geometric progression).

operations.name: gradient
operations.summary: Return the gradient of an N-dimensional array.

operations.name: greater
operations.summary: Return the truth value of (x1 > x2) element-wise.

operations.name: greater_equal
operations.summary: Return the truth value of (x1 >= x2) element-wise.

operations.name: hamming
operations.notes: Hamming window. Cost: n (one cosine per sample).
operations.summary: Return the Hamming window.

operations.name: hanning
operations.notes: Hanning window. Cost: n (one cosine per sample).
operations.summary: Return the Hanning window.

operations.name: heaviside
operations.summary: Compute the Heaviside step function.

operations.name: histogram
operations.summary: Compute the histogram of a dataset.

operations.name: histogram2d
operations.summary: Compute the bi-dimensional histogram of two data samples.

operations.name: histogram_bin_edges
operations.summary: Function to calculate only the edges of the bins used by the `histogram` function.

operations.name: histogramdd
operations.summary: Compute the multidimensional histogram of some data.

operations.name: hsplit
operations.summary: Split an array into multiple sub-arrays horizontally (column-wise).

operations.name: hstack
operations.summary: Stack arrays in sequence horizontally (column wise).

operations.name: hypot
operations.summary: Given the "legs" of a right triangle, return its hypotenuse.

operations.name: i0
operations.notes: Modified Bessel function of order 0, element-wise.
operations.summary: Modified Bessel function of the first kind, order 0.

operations.name: identity
operations.summary: Return the identity array.

operations.name: imag
operations.notes: Return imaginary part of complex array.
operations.summary: Return the imaginary part of the complex argument.

operations.name: in1d
operations.notes: Set membership; cost = (n+m)*ceil(log2(n+m)). Removed in numpy 2.4; use `isin` instead.
operations.summary: Test whether each element of a 1-D array is also present in a second array.

operations.name: indices
operations.notes: Return array representing indices of a grid. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Return an array representing the indices of a grid.

operations.name: inner
operations.summary: Inner product of two arrays.

operations.name: insert
operations.notes: Insert values along axis before given indices. Cost: numel(values).
operations.summary: Insert values along the given axis before the given indices.

operations.name: interp
operations.summary: One-dimensional linear interpolation for monotonically increasing sample points.

operations.name: intersect1d
operations.summary: Find the intersection of two arrays.

operations.name: invert
operations.summary: Compute bit-wise inversion, or bit-wise NOT, element-wise.

operations.name: isclose
operations.summary: Returns a boolean array where two arrays are element-wise equal within a tolerance.

operations.name: iscomplex
operations.notes: Test if element is complex element-wise.
operations.summary: Returns a bool array, where True if input element is complex.

operations.name: iscomplexobj
operations.notes: Return True if input is a complex type or array.
operations.summary: Check for a complex type or an array of complex numbers.

operations.name: isdtype
operations.notes: Return True if array or dtype is of specified kind (NumPy 2.x).
operations.summary: Determine if a provided dtype is of a specified data type ``kind``.

operations.name: isfinite
operations.notes: Test for finite values element-wise. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Test element-wise for finiteness (not infinity and not Not a Number).

operations.name: isfortran
operations.notes: Return True if array is Fortran contiguous.
operations.summary: Check if the array is Fortran contiguous but *not* C contiguous.

operations.name: isin
operations.summary: Calculates ``element in test_elements``, broadcasting over `element` only. Returns a boolean array of the same shape as `element` that is True where an element of `element` is in `test_elements` and False otherwise.

operations.name: isinf
operations.notes: Test for infinity element-wise. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Test element-wise for positive or negative infinity.

operations.name: isnan
operations.notes: Test for NaN element-wise. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Test element-wise for NaN and return result as a boolean array.

operations.name: isneginf
operations.summary: Test element-wise for negative infinity, return result as bool array.

operations.name: isposinf
operations.summary: Test element-wise for positive infinity, return result as bool array.

operations.name: isreal
operations.notes: Test if element is real (imag == 0) element-wise.
operations.summary: Returns a bool array, where True if input element is real.

operations.name: isrealobj
operations.notes: Return True if x is a not complex type or array.
operations.summary: Return True if x is a not complex type or an array of complex numbers.

operations.name: isscalar
operations.notes: Return True if input is a scalar.
operations.summary: Returns True if the type of `element` is a scalar type.

operations.name: issubdtype
operations.notes: Return True if first argument is lower in type hierarchy.
operations.summary: Returns True if first argument is a typecode lower/equal in type hierarchy.

operations.name: iterable
operations.notes: Return True if object is iterable.
operations.summary: Check whether or not an object can be iterated over.

operations.name: ix_
operations.notes: Construct open mesh from multiple sequences. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Construct an open mesh from multiple sequences.

operations.name: kaiser
operations.notes: Kaiser window. Cost: 3*n (Bessel function eval per sample).
operations.summary: Return the Kaiser window.

operations.name: kron
operations.notes: Kronecker product; cost proportional to output size.
operations.summary: Kronecker product of two arrays.

operations.name: lcm
operations.summary: Returns the lowest common multiple of ``|x1|`` and ``|x2|``

operations.name: ldexp
operations.summary: Returns x1 * 2**x2, element-wise.

operations.name: left_shift
operations.summary: Shift the bits of an integer to the left.

operations.name: less
operations.summary: Return the truth value of (x1 < x2) element-wise.

operations.name: less_equal
operations.summary: Return the truth value of (x1 <= x2) element-wise.

operations.name: lexsort
operations.summary: Perform an indirect stable sort using a sequence of keys.

operations.name: linalg.cholesky
operations.summary: Cholesky decomposition.

operations.name: linalg.cond
operations.summary: Compute the condition number of a matrix.

operations.name: linalg.cross
operations.summary: Returns the cross product of 3-element vectors.

operations.name: linalg.det
operations.summary: Compute the determinant of an array.

operations.name: linalg.diagonal
operations.notes: View of diagonal — delegates to whest.diagonal. Cost: 0 FLOPs.
operations.summary: Returns specified diagonals of a matrix (or a stack of matrices) ``x``.

operations.name: linalg.eig
operations.summary: Compute the eigenvalues and right eigenvectors of a square array.

operations.name: linalg.eigh
operations.summary: Return the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a complex Hermitian (conjugate symmetric) or a real symmetric matrix.

operations.name: linalg.eigvals
operations.summary: Compute the eigenvalues of a general matrix.

operations.name: linalg.eigvalsh
operations.summary: Compute the eigenvalues of a complex Hermitian or real symmetric matrix.

operations.name: linalg.inv
operations.summary: Compute the inverse of a matrix.

operations.name: linalg.lstsq
operations.summary: Return the least-squares solution to a linear matrix equation.

operations.name: linalg.matmul
operations.summary: Computes the matrix product.

operations.name: linalg.matrix_norm
operations.notes: Matrix norm. Cost depends on ord: 2*numel for Frobenius, m*n*min(m,n) for ord=2.
operations.summary: Computes the matrix norm of a matrix (or a stack of matrices) ``x``.

operations.name: linalg.matrix_power
operations.notes: Matrix power. Cost: $(\lfloor\log_2 k\rfloor + \text{popcount}(k) - 1) \cdot n^3$ (exponentiation by squaring).
operations.summary: Raise a square matrix to the (integer) power `n`.

operations.name: linalg.matrix_rank
operations.summary: Return matrix rank of array using SVD method

operations.name: linalg.matrix_transpose
operations.notes: Transpose view — delegates to whest.matrix_transpose. Cost: 0 FLOPs.
operations.summary: Transposes a matrix (or a stack of matrices) ``x``.

operations.name: linalg.multi_dot
operations.notes: Chain matmul. Cost: sum of optimal chain matmul costs (CLRS §15.2).
operations.summary: Compute the dot product of two or more arrays in a single function call, while automatically selecting the fastest evaluation order.

operations.name: linalg.norm
operations.notes: Norm. Cost depends on ord: numel for L1/inf, 2*numel for Frobenius, m*n*min(m,n) for ord=2.
operations.summary: Matrix or vector norm.

operations.name: linalg.outer
operations.summary: Compute the outer product of two vectors.

operations.name: linalg.pinv
operations.summary: Compute the (Moore-Penrose) pseudo-inverse of a matrix.

operations.name: linalg.qr
operations.summary: Compute the qr factorization of a matrix.

operations.name: linalg.slogdet
operations.summary: Compute the sign and (natural) logarithm of the determinant of an array.

operations.name: linalg.solve
operations.summary: Solve a linear matrix equation, or system of linear scalar equations.

operations.name: linalg.svd
operations.summary: Singular Value Decomposition.

operations.name: linalg.svdvals
operations.summary: Returns the singular values of a matrix (or a stack of matrices) ``x``. When x is a stack of matrices, the function will compute the singular values for each matrix in the stack.

operations.name: linalg.tensordot
operations.notes: Delegates to `we.tensordot` which charges FLOPs based on contraction.
operations.summary: Compute tensor dot product along specified axes.

operations.name: linalg.tensorinv
operations.notes: Tensor inverse. Cost: $n^3$ after reshape (delegates to inv).
operations.summary: Compute the 'inverse' of an N-dimensional array.

operations.name: linalg.tensorsolve
operations.notes: Tensor solve. Cost: $n^3$ after reshape (delegates to solve).
operations.summary: Solve the tensor equation ``a x = b`` for x.

operations.name: linalg.trace
operations.notes: Matrix trace. Cost: n (sum of diagonal elements).
operations.summary: Returns the sum along the specified diagonals of a matrix (or a stack of matrices) ``x``.

operations.name: linalg.vecdot
operations.summary: Computes the vector dot product.

operations.name: linalg.vector_norm
operations.notes: Vector norm. Cost: numel (or 2*numel for general p-norm).
operations.summary: Computes the vector norm of a vector (or batch of vectors) ``x``.

operations.name: linspace
operations.notes: Return evenly spaced numbers over interval. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Return evenly spaced numbers over a specified interval.

operations.name: log
operations.summary: Natural logarithm, element-wise.

operations.name: log10
operations.summary: Return the base 10 logarithm of the input array, element-wise.

operations.name: log1p
operations.summary: Return the natural logarithm of one plus the input array, element-wise.

operations.name: log2
operations.summary: Base-2 logarithm of `x`.

operations.name: logaddexp
operations.summary: Logarithm of the sum of exponentiations of the inputs.

operations.name: logaddexp2
operations.summary: Logarithm of the sum of exponentiations of the inputs in base-2.

operations.name: logical_and
operations.summary: Compute the truth value of x1 AND x2 element-wise.

operations.name: logical_not
operations.summary: Compute the truth value of NOT x element-wise.

operations.name: logical_or
operations.summary: Compute the truth value of x1 OR x2 element-wise.

operations.name: logical_xor
operations.summary: Compute the truth value of x1 XOR x2, element-wise.

operations.name: logspace
operations.summary: Return numbers spaced evenly on a log scale.

operations.name: mask_indices
operations.notes: Return indices of mask for n x n array. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Return the indices to access (n, n) arrays, given a masking function.

operations.name: matmul
operations.summary: Matrix product of two arrays.

operations.name: matrix_transpose
operations.notes: Transpose last two dimensions (NumPy 2.x array API).
operations.summary: Transposes a matrix (or a stack of matrices) ``x``.

operations.name: matvec
operations.summary: Matrix-vector dot product of two arrays.

operations.name: max
operations.summary: Return the maximum of an array or maximum along an axis.

operations.name: maximum
operations.summary: Element-wise maximum of array elements.

operations.name: may_share_memory
operations.notes: Determine if two arrays might share memory.
operations.summary: Determine if two arrays might share memory

operations.name: mean
operations.summary: Compute the arithmetic mean along the specified axis.

operations.name: median
operations.summary: Compute the median along the specified axis.

operations.name: meshgrid
operations.notes: Coordinate matrices from coordinate vectors. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Return a tuple of coordinate matrices from coordinate vectors.

operations.name: min
operations.summary: Return the minimum of an array or minimum along an axis.

operations.name: min_scalar_type
operations.notes: Return the minimum scalar type for a value.
operations.summary: For scalar ``a``, returns the data type with the smallest size and smallest scalar kind which can hold its value.  For non-scalar array ``a``, returns the vector's dtype unmodified.

operations.name: minimum
operations.summary: Element-wise minimum of array elements.

operations.name: mintypecode
operations.notes: Return minimum data type character that can satisfy all given types.
operations.summary: Return the character for the minimum-size type to which given types can be safely cast.

operations.name: mod
operations.summary: Returns the element-wise remainder of division.

operations.name: modf
operations.notes: Return fractional and integral parts element-wise.
operations.summary: Return the fractional and integral parts of an array, element-wise.

operations.name: moveaxis
operations.summary: Move axes of an array to new positions.

operations.name: multiply
operations.summary: Multiply arguments element-wise.

operations.name: nan_to_num
operations.notes: Replace NaN/inf with finite numbers element-wise.
operations.summary: Replace NaN with zero and infinity with large finite numbers (default behaviour) or with the numbers defined by the user using the `nan`, `posinf` and/or `neginf` keywords.

operations.name: nanargmax
operations.summary: Return the indices of the maximum values in the specified axis ignoring NaNs. For all-NaN slices ``ValueError`` is raised. Warning: the results cannot be trusted if a slice contains only NaNs and -Infs.

operations.name: nanargmin
operations.summary: Return the indices of the minimum values in the specified axis ignoring NaNs. For all-NaN slices ``ValueError`` is raised. Warning: the results cannot be trusted if a slice contains only NaNs and Infs.

operations.name: nancumprod
operations.summary: Return the cumulative product of array elements over a given axis treating Not a Numbers (NaNs) as one.  The cumulative product does not change when NaNs are encountered and leading NaNs are replaced by ones.

operations.name: nancumsum
operations.summary: Return the cumulative sum of array elements over a given axis treating Not a Numbers (NaNs) as zero.  The cumulative sum does not change when NaNs are encountered and leading NaNs are replaced by zeros.

operations.name: nanmax
operations.summary: Return the maximum of an array or maximum along an axis, ignoring any NaNs.  When all-NaN slices are encountered a ``RuntimeWarning`` is raised and NaN is returned for that slice.

operations.name: nanmean
operations.summary: Compute the arithmetic mean along the specified axis, ignoring NaNs.

operations.name: nanmedian
operations.summary: Compute the median along the specified axis, while ignoring NaNs.

operations.name: nanmin
operations.summary: Return minimum of an array or minimum along an axis, ignoring any NaNs. When all-NaN slices are encountered a ``RuntimeWarning`` is raised and Nan is returned for that slice.

operations.name: nanpercentile
operations.summary: Compute the qth percentile of the data along the specified axis, while ignoring nan values.

operations.name: nanprod
operations.summary: Return the product of array elements over a given axis treating Not a Numbers (NaNs) as ones.

operations.name: nanquantile
operations.summary: Compute the qth quantile of the data along the specified axis, while ignoring nan values. Returns the qth quantile(s) of the array elements.

operations.name: nanstd
operations.summary: Compute the standard deviation along the specified axis, while ignoring NaNs.

operations.name: nansum
operations.summary: Return the sum of array elements over a given axis treating Not a Numbers (NaNs) as zero.

operations.name: nanvar
operations.summary: Compute the variance along the specified axis, while ignoring NaNs.

operations.name: ndim
operations.notes: Return number of dimensions of array.
operations.summary: Return the number of dimensions of an array.

operations.name: negative
operations.summary: Numerical negative, element-wise.

operations.name: nextafter
operations.notes: Return next float after x1 toward x2 element-wise.
operations.summary: Return the next floating-point value after x1 towards x2, element-wise.

operations.name: nonzero
operations.notes: Return indices of non-zero elements. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Return the indices of the elements that are non-zero.

operations.name: not_equal
operations.summary: Return (x1 != x2) element-wise.

operations.name: ones
operations.summary: Return a new array of given shape and type, filled with ones.

operations.name: ones_like
operations.notes: Array of ones with same shape/type as input.
operations.summary: Return an array of ones with the same shape and type as a given array.

operations.name: outer
operations.notes: Outer product of two vectors; cost = M*N.
operations.summary: Compute the outer product of two vectors.

operations.name: packbits
operations.notes: Pack elements of array into bits. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Packs the elements of a binary-valued array into bits in a uint8 array.

operations.name: pad
operations.summary: Pad an array.

operations.name: partition
operations.summary: Return a partitioned copy of an array.

operations.name: percentile
operations.summary: Compute the q-th percentile of the data along the specified axis.

operations.name: permute_dims
operations.summary: Returns an array with axes transposed.

operations.name: piecewise
operations.summary: Evaluate a piecewise-defined function.

operations.name: place
operations.notes: Change elements satisfying condition. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Change elements of an array based on conditional and input values.

operations.name: poly
operations.summary: Find the coefficients of a polynomial with the given sequence of roots.

operations.name: polyadd
operations.notes: Add two polynomials. Cost: max(n1, n2) FLOPs.
operations.summary: Find the sum of two polynomials.

operations.name: polyder
operations.summary: Return the derivative of the specified order of a polynomial.

operations.name: polydiv
operations.notes: Divide one polynomial by another. Cost: n1 * n2 FLOPs.
operations.summary: Returns the quotient and remainder of polynomial division.

operations.name: polyfit
operations.notes: Least squares polynomial fit. Cost: 2 * m * (deg+1)^2 FLOPs.
operations.summary: Least squares polynomial fit.

operations.name: polyint
operations.summary: Return an antiderivative (indefinite integral) of a polynomial.

operations.name: polymul
operations.notes: Multiply polynomials. Cost: n1 * n2 FLOPs.
operations.summary: Find the product of two polynomials.

operations.name: polysub
operations.notes: Difference (subtraction) of two polynomials. Cost: max(n1, n2) FLOPs.
operations.summary: Difference (subtraction) of two polynomials.

operations.name: polyval
operations.notes: Evaluate polynomial at given points. Cost: $m \cdot \text{deg}$ (Horner's method, FMA=1).
operations.summary: Evaluate a polynomial at specific values.

operations.name: positive
operations.notes: Element-wise unary plus (copy with sign preserved).
operations.summary: Numerical positive, element-wise.

operations.name: power
operations.summary: First array elements raised to powers from second array, element-wise.

operations.name: prod
operations.summary: Return the product of array elements over a given axis.

operations.name: promote_types
operations.notes: Return smallest type to which both types may be safely cast.
operations.summary: Returns the data type with the smallest size and smallest scalar kind to which both ``type1`` and ``type2`` may be safely cast. The returned data type is always considered "canonical", this mainly means that the promoted dtype will always be in native byte order.

operations.name: ptp
operations.summary: Range of values (maximum - minimum) along an axis.

operations.name: put
operations.notes: Replace elements at given flat indices. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Replaces specified elements of an array with given values.

operations.name: put_along_axis
operations.notes: Put values into destination array using indices. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Put values into the destination array by matching 1d index and data slices.

operations.name: putmask
operations.notes: Change elements of array based on condition and input values. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Changes elements of an array based on conditional and input values.

operations.name: quantile
operations.summary: Compute the q-th quantile of the data along the specified axis.

operations.name: radians
operations.summary: Convert angles from degrees to radians.

operations.name: random.beta
operations.summary: Draw samples from a Beta distribution.

operations.name: random.binomial
operations.summary: Draw samples from a binomial distribution.

operations.name: random.bytes
operations.summary: Return random bytes.

operations.name: random.chisquare
operations.summary: Draw samples from a chi-square distribution.

operations.name: random.choice
operations.notes: Sampling; cost = numel(output) if replace, n*ceil(log2(n)) if not.
operations.summary: Generates a random sample from a given 1-D array

operations.name: random.default_rng
operations.notes: Construct a new Generator with default BitGenerator.
operations.summary: Construct a new Generator with the default BitGenerator (PCG64).

operations.name: random.dirichlet
operations.summary: Draw samples from the Dirichlet distribution.

operations.name: random.exponential
operations.summary: Draw samples from an exponential distribution.

operations.name: random.f
operations.summary: Draw samples from an F distribution.

operations.name: random.gamma
operations.summary: Draw samples from a Gamma distribution.

operations.name: random.geometric
operations.summary: Draw samples from the geometric distribution.

operations.name: random.get_state
operations.notes: Return tuple representing internal state of generator.
operations.summary: Return a tuple representing the internal state of the generator.

operations.name: random.gumbel
operations.summary: Draw samples from a Gumbel distribution.

operations.name: random.hypergeometric
operations.summary: Draw samples from a Hypergeometric distribution.

operations.name: random.laplace
operations.summary: Draw samples from the Laplace or double exponential distribution with specified location (or mean) and scale (decay).

operations.name: random.logistic
operations.summary: Draw samples from a logistic distribution.

operations.name: random.lognormal
operations.summary: Draw samples from a log-normal distribution.

operations.name: random.logseries
operations.summary: Draw samples from a logarithmic series distribution.

operations.name: random.multinomial
operations.summary: Draw samples from a multinomial distribution.

operations.name: random.multivariate_normal
operations.summary: Draw random samples from a multivariate normal distribution.

operations.name: random.negative_binomial
operations.summary: Draw samples from a negative binomial distribution.

operations.name: random.noncentral_chisquare
operations.summary: Draw samples from a noncentral chi-square distribution.

operations.name: random.noncentral_f
operations.summary: Draw samples from the noncentral F distribution.

operations.name: random.normal
operations.summary: Draw random samples from a normal (Gaussian) distribution.

operations.name: random.pareto
operations.summary: Draw samples from a Pareto II or Lomax distribution with specified shape.

operations.name: random.permutation
operations.summary: Randomly permute a sequence, or return a permuted range.

operations.name: random.poisson
operations.summary: Draw samples from a Poisson distribution.

operations.name: random.power
operations.summary: Draws samples in [0, 1] from a power distribution with positive exponent a - 1.

operations.name: random.rand
operations.summary: Random values in a given shape.

operations.name: random.randint
operations.summary: Return random integers from `low` (inclusive) to `high` (exclusive).

operations.name: random.randn
operations.summary: Return a sample (or samples) from the "standard normal" distribution.

operations.name: random.random
operations.summary: Return random floats in the half-open interval [0.0, 1.0). Alias for `random_sample` to ease forward-porting to the new random API.

operations.name: random.random_integers
operations.summary: Random integers of type `we.int_` between `low` and `high`, inclusive.

operations.name: random.random_sample
operations.summary: Return random floats in the half-open interval [0.0, 1.0).

operations.name: random.rayleigh
operations.summary: Draw samples from a Rayleigh distribution.

operations.name: random.seed
operations.summary: Reseed the singleton RandomState instance.

operations.name: random.set_state
operations.summary: Set the internal state of the generator from a tuple.

operations.name: random.shuffle
operations.summary: Modify a sequence in-place by shuffling its contents.

operations.name: random.standard_cauchy
operations.summary: Draw samples from a standard Cauchy distribution with mode = 0.

operations.name: random.standard_exponential
operations.summary: Draw samples from the standard exponential distribution.

operations.name: random.standard_gamma
operations.summary: Draw samples from a standard Gamma distribution.

operations.name: random.standard_normal
operations.summary: Draw samples from a standard Normal distribution (mean=0, stdev=1).

operations.name: random.standard_t
operations.summary: Draw samples from a standard Student's t distribution with `df` degrees of freedom.

operations.name: random.triangular
operations.summary: Draw samples from the triangular distribution over the interval ``[left, right]``.

operations.name: random.uniform
operations.summary: Draw samples from a uniform distribution.

operations.name: random.vonmises
operations.summary: Draw samples from a von Mises distribution.

operations.name: random.wald
operations.summary: Draw samples from a Wald, or inverse Gaussian, distribution.

operations.name: random.weibull
operations.summary: Draw samples from a Weibull distribution.

operations.name: random.zipf
operations.summary: Draw samples from a Zipf distribution.

operations.name: ravel
operations.notes: Return contiguous flattened array. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Return a contiguous flattened array.

operations.name: ravel_multi_index
operations.notes: Convert multi-dimensional index to flat index.
operations.summary: Converts a tuple of index arrays into an array of flat indices, applying boundary modes to the multi-index.

operations.name: real
operations.notes: Return real part of complex array.
operations.summary: Return the real part of the complex argument.

operations.name: real_if_close
operations.notes: Return real array if imaginary part is negligible.
operations.summary: If input is complex with all imaginary parts close to zero, return real parts.

operations.name: reciprocal
operations.summary: Return the reciprocal of the argument, element-wise.

operations.name: remainder
operations.summary: Returns the element-wise remainder of division.

operations.name: repeat
operations.notes: Repeat elements of an array. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Repeat each element of an array after themselves

operations.name: require
operations.summary: Return an ndarray of the provided type that satisfies requirements.

operations.name: reshape
operations.summary: Gives a new shape to an array without changing its data.

operations.name: resize
operations.notes: Return new array with given shape by repeating. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Return a new array with the specified shape.

operations.name: result_type
operations.notes: Return type that results from applying NumPy type promotion.
operations.summary: Returns the type that results from applying the NumPy type promotion rules to the arguments.

operations.name: right_shift
operations.summary: Shift the bits of an integer to the right.

operations.name: rint
operations.summary: Round elements of the array to the nearest integer.

operations.name: roll
operations.notes: Roll array elements along axis. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Roll array elements along a given axis.

operations.name: rollaxis
operations.notes: Roll specified axis backwards. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Roll the specified axis backwards, until it lies in a given position.

operations.name: roots
operations.notes: Return roots of polynomial with given coefficients. Cost: $n^3$ (companion matrix eig, simplified).
operations.summary: Return the roots of a polynomial with coefficients given in p.

operations.name: rot90
operations.summary: Rotate an array by 90 degrees in the plane specified by axes.

operations.name: searchsorted
operations.summary: Find indices where elements should be inserted to maintain order.

operations.name: select
operations.notes: Return array from list of choices based on conditions. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Return an array drawn from elements in choicelist, depending on conditions.

operations.name: setdiff1d
operations.summary: Find the set difference of two arrays.

operations.name: setxor1d
operations.summary: Find the set exclusive-or of two arrays.

operations.name: shape
operations.summary: Return the shape of an array.

operations.name: shares_memory
operations.notes: Determine if two arrays share memory.
operations.summary: Determine if two arrays share memory.

operations.name: sign
operations.summary: Returns an element-wise indication of the sign of a number.

operations.name: signbit
operations.notes: Returns True for elements with negative sign bit.
operations.summary: Returns element-wise True where signbit is set (less than zero).

operations.name: sin
operations.summary: Trigonometric sine, element-wise.

operations.name: sinc
operations.summary: Return the normalized sinc function.

operations.name: sinh
operations.summary: Hyperbolic sine, element-wise.

operations.name: size
operations.notes: Return number of elements in array.
operations.summary: Return the number of elements along a given axis.

operations.name: sort
operations.notes: Comparison sort; cost = n*ceil(log2(n)) per slice.
operations.summary: Return a sorted copy of an array.

operations.name: sort_complex
operations.summary: Sort a complex array using the real part first, then the imaginary part.

operations.name: spacing
operations.notes: Return ULP spacing for each element.
operations.summary: Return the distance between x and the nearest adjacent number.

operations.name: split
operations.notes: Split array into sub-arrays. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Split an array into multiple sub-arrays as views into `ary`.

operations.name: sqrt
operations.summary: Return the non-negative square-root of an array, element-wise.

operations.name: square
operations.summary: Return the element-wise square of the input.

operations.name: squeeze
operations.summary: Remove axes of length one from `a`.

operations.name: stack
operations.notes: Join arrays along new axis. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Join a sequence of arrays along a new axis.

operations.name: stats.cauchy.cdf
operations.summary: Cumulative distribution function of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.cauchy.pdf
operations.summary: Probability density function at x of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.cauchy.ppf
operations.summary: Percent point function (inverse of `cdf`) at q of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.expon.cdf
operations.summary: Cumulative distribution function of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.expon.pdf
operations.summary: Probability density function at x of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.expon.ppf
operations.summary: Percent point function (inverse of `cdf`) at q of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.laplace.cdf
operations.summary: Cumulative distribution function of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.laplace.pdf
operations.summary: Probability density function at x of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.laplace.ppf
operations.summary: Percent point function (inverse of `cdf`) at q of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.logistic.cdf
operations.summary: Cumulative distribution function of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.logistic.pdf
operations.summary: Probability density function at x of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.logistic.ppf
operations.summary: Percent point function (inverse of `cdf`) at q of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.lognorm.cdf
operations.summary: Cumulative distribution function of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.lognorm.pdf
operations.summary: Probability density function at x of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.lognorm.ppf
operations.summary: Percent point function (inverse of `cdf`) at q of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.norm.cdf
operations.summary: Cumulative distribution function of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.norm.pdf
operations.summary: Probability density function at x of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.norm.ppf
operations.summary: Percent point function (inverse of `cdf`) at q of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.truncnorm.cdf
operations.summary: Cumulative distribution function of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.truncnorm.pdf
operations.summary: Probability density function at x of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.truncnorm.ppf
operations.summary: Percent point function (inverse of `cdf`) at q of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.uniform.cdf
operations.summary: Cumulative distribution function of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.uniform.pdf
operations.summary: Probability density function at x of the given RV.

operations.name: stats.uniform.ppf
operations.summary: Percent point function (inverse of `cdf`) at q of the given RV.

operations.name: std
operations.summary: Compute the standard deviation along the specified axis.

operations.name: subtract
operations.summary: Subtract arguments, element-wise.

operations.name: sum
operations.summary: Sum of array elements over a given axis.

operations.name: swapaxes
operations.notes: Interchange two axes of an array.
operations.summary: Interchange two axes of an array.

operations.name: take
operations.notes: Take elements from array along axis. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Take elements from an array along an axis.

operations.name: take_along_axis
operations.notes: Take values from input array by matching 1-D index. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Take values from the input array by matching 1d index and data slices.

operations.name: tan
operations.summary: Compute tangent element-wise.

operations.name: tanh
operations.summary: Compute hyperbolic tangent element-wise.

operations.cost_formula: product of contracted dims * output size
operations.name: tensordot
operations.notes: Tensor dot product along specified axes.
operations.summary: Compute tensor dot product along specified axes.

operations.name: tile
operations.notes: Repeat array by tiling. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Construct an array by repeating A the number of times given by reps.

operations.name: trace
operations.summary: Return the sum along diagonals of the array.

operations.name: transpose
operations.summary: Returns an array with axes transposed.

operations.name: trapezoid
operations.summary: Integrate along the given axis using the composite trapezoidal rule.

operations.name: tri
operations.notes: Array with ones at and below given diagonal.
operations.summary: An array with ones at and below the given diagonal and zeros elsewhere.

operations.name: tril
operations.summary: Lower triangle of an array.

operations.name: tril_indices
operations.notes: Return lower-triangle indices for n x n array.
operations.summary: Return the indices for the lower-triangle of an (n, m) array.

operations.name: tril_indices_from
operations.notes: Return lower-triangle indices for given array.
operations.summary: Return the indices for the lower-triangle of arr.

operations.name: trim_zeros
operations.notes: Trim leading/trailing zeros from 1-D array. Cost: num trimmed.
operations.summary: Remove values along a dimension which are zero along all other.

operations.name: triu
operations.summary: Upper triangle of an array.

operations.name: triu_indices
operations.notes: Return upper-triangle indices for n x n array.
operations.summary: Return the indices for the upper-triangle of an (n, m) array.

operations.name: triu_indices_from
operations.notes: Return upper-triangle indices for given array.
operations.summary: Return the indices for the upper-triangle of arr.

operations.name: true_divide
operations.summary: Divide arguments element-wise.

operations.name: trunc
operations.summary: Return the truncated value of the input, element-wise.

operations.name: typename
operations.notes: Return description of given data type code.
operations.summary: Return a description for the given data type code.

operations.name: union1d
operations.summary: Find the union of two arrays.

operations.name: unique
operations.summary: Find the unique elements of an array.

operations.name: unique_all
operations.summary: Find the unique elements of an array, and counts, inverse, and indices.

operations.name: unique_counts
operations.summary: Find the unique elements and counts of an input array `x`.

operations.name: unique_inverse
operations.summary: Find the unique elements of `x` and indices to reconstruct `x`.

operations.name: unique_values
operations.summary: Returns the unique elements of an input array `x`.

operations.name: unpackbits
operations.notes: Unpack elements of array into bits. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Unpacks elements of a uint8 array into a binary-valued output array.

operations.name: unravel_index
operations.notes: Convert flat index to multi-dimensional index.
operations.summary: Converts a flat index or array of flat indices into a tuple of coordinate arrays.

operations.name: unstack
operations.notes: Unstack array along axis into tuple of arrays (NumPy 2.x). Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Split an array into a sequence of arrays along the given axis.

operations.name: unwrap
operations.summary: Unwrap by taking the complement of large deltas with respect to the period.

operations.name: vander
operations.summary: Generate a Vandermonde matrix.

operations.name: var
operations.summary: Compute the variance along the specified axis.

operations.name: vdot
operations.notes: Dot product with conjugation; cost = N (FMA=1).
operations.summary: Return the dot product of two vectors.

operations.name: vecdot
operations.notes: Vector dot product along last axis.
operations.summary: Vector dot product of two arrays.

operations.name: vecmat
operations.summary: Vector-matrix dot product of two arrays.

operations.name: vsplit
operations.notes: Split array into rows. Cost: numel(output).
operations.summary: Split an array into multiple sub-arrays vertically (row-wise).

operations.name: vstack
operations.summary: Stack arrays in sequence vertically (row wise).

operations.name: where
operations.notes: Select elements based on condition. Cost: numel(input).
operations.summary: Return elements chosen from `x` or `y` depending on `condition`.

operations.name: zeros
operations.summary: Return a new array of given shape and type, filled with zeros.

operations.name: zeros_like
operations.notes: Array of zeros with same shape/type as input.
operations.summary: Return an array of zeros with the same shape and type as a given array.
